text:
Google released [Gemma 4](https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/technology/developers-tools/gemma-4/) today. Here is Sebastian Raschka's take on it, complete with a nice architectural diagram. EDIT: Nathan Lambert also did a good [write up](https://www.interconnects.ai/p/gemma-4-and-what-makes-an-open-model). 
> Architecture-wise, besides multi-model support, Gemma 4 (31B) looks pretty much unchanged compared to Gemma 3 (27B).

> Gemma 4 maintains a relatively unique Pre- and Post-norm setup and remains relatively classic, with a 5:1 hybrid attention mechanism combining a sliding-window (local) layer and a full-attention (global) layer. The attention mechanism itself is also classic Grouped Query Attention (GQA).

> But let's not be fooled by the lack of architectural changes. Looking at the benchmarks, Gemma 4 is a huge leap from Gemma 3. This is likely due to the training set and recipe. 

> Overall, it's a nice and strong model release and a strong contender for local usage. Also, one aspect that should not be underrated is that (it seems) the model is now released with a standard Apache 2.0 open-source license.

preview.title:
Flagship open-weight release days are always exciting. Was just reading through the Gemma 4 reports, configs, and code, and here are my takeaways:
Architecture-wise, besides multi-model support… | Sebastian Raschka, PhD
preview.description:
Flagship open-weight release days are always exciting. Was just reading through the Gemma 4 reports, configs, and code, and here are my takeaways:
Architecture-wise, besides multi-model support, Gemma 4 (31B) looks pretty much unchanged compared to Gemma 3 (27B). Gemma 4 maintains a relatively unique Pre- and Post-norm setup and remains relatively classic, with a 5:1 hybrid attention mechanism combining a sliding-window (local) layer and a full-attention (global) layer. The attention mechanism itself is also classic Grouped Query Attention (GQA).
But let’s not be fooled by the lack of architectural changes. Looking at the benchmarks, Gemma 4 is a huge leap from Gemma 3. This is likely due to the training set and recipe. Interestingly, on the AI Arena Leaderboard, Gemma 4 (31B) ranks similarly to the much larger Qwen3.5-397B-A17B model. But as I discussed in my model evaluation article, arena scores are a bit problematic as they can be gamed and are biased towards human (style) preference. If we look at some other common benchmarks, which I plotted below, we can see that it’s indeed a very clear leap over Gemma 3 and ranks on par with Qwen3.5 27B. Note that there is also a Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) Gemma 4 variant that is slightly smaller (27B with 4 billion parameters active. The benchmarks are only slightly worse compared to Gemma 4 (31B). I omitted the MoE architecture in the figure below because the figure is already very crowded, but you can find it in my LLM Architecture Gallery.
Anyways, overall, it’s a nice and strong model release and a strong contender for local usage. Also, one aspect that should not be underrated is that (it seems) the model is now released with a standard Apache 2.0 open-source license, which has much friendlier usage terms than the custom Gemma 3 license.

text:
Generalist's latest milestone in scaling robot learning: **GEN-1**: Scaling Embodied Foundation Models to Mastery. [[Blog](https://generalistai.com/blog/apr-02-2026-GEN-1)]
> GEN-1 can begin to master simple tasks - on several tasks the model now exceeds 99% success rates (reliability), can complete tasks up to ~3x faster than the prior SOTA (speed), and exhibits a broad range of emergent behaviors to recover in unexpected scenarios (improvisation). In each case, these results require only approximately 1 hr of robot data.

preview.title: Introducing GEN-1

text:
In this episode of Automated, [Ranjay Krishna](https://www.ranjaykrishna.com/index.html) explains why robots struggle with real-world tasks, from learning by watching humans to adapting to unpredictable environments.

Ranjay breaks down the core challenges in robotics today, including the sim-to-real gap, the lack of diverse training data, and why errors compound over time when robots interact with the physical world.

preview.title: Why Robots Still Fail in the Real World

text: Artemis II is on target for a launch from the Kennedy Space center tonight. It's the first crewed lunar mission since Apollo 17 in 1972! 🚀

preview.title: NASA's Artemis II Crew Launches To The Moon (Official Broadcast)

text: New work out of Jim Fan's group at NVIDIA: **CaP-X**: A Framework for Benchmarking and Improving Coding Agents for Robot Manipulation. [[Code](https://github.com/capgym/cap-x)] [[Paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.22435)]
A new effort to create a comprehensive and up-to-date repository of open-source assets for robot manipulation.

Today HuggingFace released v1.0 of their TRL (Transformers Reinforcement Learning) post training library.

> 75+ methods. SFT, DPO, GRPO, async RL to take advantage of the latest and greatest open-source. 6 years from first commit to the library that post-trains most open models in the world. Built to be future proof.

preview.title: TRL v1.0: Post-Training Library Built to Move with the Field

text:
How [Ilia Larchenko](https://huggingface.co/IliaLarchenko) et al. trained a robot to do 50 household tasks in simulation by building upon the [$\pi_{0.5}$](https://www.pi.website/blog/pi05) VLA architecture [[Code](https://github.com/IliaLarchenko/behavior-1k-solution)] [[Paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.06951)] [[Video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=J4wpO0EdCZs)]

NVIDIA's [Openpi Comet](https://github.com/mli0603/openpi-comet) came in second place, but they were able to push performance significantly higher on the public validation set after the fact.

preview.title: Winning the BEHAVIOR-1K Challenge (NeurIPS 2025)
preview.description: Can one robotics model trained via Imitation Learning solve 50 household tasks?

text: **Dream2Flow**, a framework that bridges video generation and robotic control through 3D object flow as an intermediate representation. This method reconstructs 3D object motions from generated videos and formulates manipulation as object trajectory tracking.

preview.title: Dream2Flow

text: Looks like TRI is building an open source VLA training framework.

preview.title: TRI Develops VLA Framework for Robotic Manipulation | Toyota Research Institute posted on the topic | LinkedIn
preview.description: TRI is developing a framework to train Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models for robotic manipulation. As we are working towards sharing this framework with the community, we have been testing on a variety of sim and real tasks. Stay tuned!
